verses.text: 'I have loved you, saith the LORD,' yet they ask, 'Wherein?' He points to His choice of Jacob over Esau. Sovereign love. God's people forget the free, distinguishing love that set its heart on them.
'I have loved you, saith the LORD,' yet they ask, 'Wherein?' He points to His choice of Jacob over Esau. Sovereign love. God's people forget the free, distinguishing love that set its heart on them.
'I have loved you, saith the LORD,' yet they ask, 'Wherein?' He points to His choice of Jacob over Esau. Sovereign love. God's people forget the free, distinguishing love that set its heart on them.
Though Edom vows to rebuild, the LORD will throw it down, calling it 'the border of wickedness.' Vain defiance. What God has cursed, no man can establish.
Though Edom vows to rebuild, the LORD will throw it down, calling it 'the border of wickedness.' Vain defiance. What God has cursed, no man can establish.
A son honours his father; 'if then I be a father, where is mine honour?' Yet they offer 'polluted bread.' Honour withheld. To bring God less than our best is to despise Him.
A son honours his father; 'if then I be a father, where is mine honour?' Yet they offer 'polluted bread.' Honour withheld. To bring God less than our best is to despise Him.
They offer the blind and lame; 'offer it now unto thy governor; will he be pleased?' Insulting gifts. What we would not dare give a man, we must not offer God.
They offer the blind and lame; 'offer it now unto thy governor; will he be pleased?' Insulting gifts. What we would not dare give a man, we must not offer God.
He takes no pleasure in such worship; yet 'from the rising of the sun... my name shall be great among the Gentiles.' A wider glory. Where Israel failed, the nations would honour God.
He takes no pleasure in such worship; yet 'from the rising of the sun... my name shall be great among the Gentiles.' A wider glory. Where Israel failed, the nations would honour God.
They call His table contemptible; cursed is the deceiver who vows and sacrifices a corrupt thing, 'for I am a great King.' Reverence due. The greatness of God forbids careless, grudging worship.
